resources.string: Prefer a dedicated keyboard? Enable the Offline Voice Input keyboard, then switch to it from your keyboard switcher when you want voice input as its own keyboard.
Display subtitles for any audio playing on your device. After starting, select \'Share entire screen\' for best results.
Automatically begin recording when the keyboard opens
Highlight transcribed text so it can be deleted with a single backspace
Keep recording when the voice keyboard is hidden; the text is inserted when you return
End the voice input popup automatically ~2 seconds after you stop speaking
How many lines the live subtitles show; older text scrolls away
Ready — in any app, tap the microphone on a supported keyboard and your speech is typed into the text field.
Microphone access is needed before voice input can work.
Almost there. When you tap your keyboard\'s mic, pick “Offline Voice Input” and choose “Always”. Tap “Try voice input” below to set it now.
It works — but no speech was detected.
Couldn\'t start voice input on this device.

How to use it
• Open any app and tap the microphone on a supported keyboard/app. A small panel slides up, you speak, tap to stop, and the text is inserted. (Enable “Auto-stop after silence” in the settings to have it stop by itself.)

Which keyboards work
• SwiftKey: works out of the box (see below if its own voice typing opens).
• HeliBoard / AnySoftKeyboard / OpenBoard: their mic key switches to the system voice keyboard — enable the “Offline Voice Input” keyboard (see “Alternative: Voice keyboard”) and it is used automatically.
• Gboard only uses Google's own voice typing and can't use this app. Use one of the keyboards above, or the built-in voice keyboard.

If your keyboard's own voice typing opens instead
• Microsoft SwiftKey: Settings → Rich input → turn off “Multi-modal voice typing”.
• Then tap the mic again and pick “Offline Voice Input”.

If Android asks which app to use
• Choose “Offline Voice Input” and tap “Always”.

If another app always opens instead
• Open Settings → Apps → that app → “Open by default” → Clear defaults, then try again.
